chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Criminal Misc: Application No.S-221 of 2026 [Mst. Shabiran Abasi vs. The State and others] Date Order with the signature of the Judge For orders on the office objection. For the hearing of the main case. 06.04.2026 Syed Ali Aamir Shah, Advocate for applicant. Mr. Imran Mobeen Khan, Assistant Prosecutor General along with ASI Muhammad Jaffar, P.C Ikhlaque Gabol, Lady P.C Asma Mahar of P.S Khanpur..-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- The alleged detainee, namely Mst. Kousar, appeared before this Court along with her minor daughter and stated that she is happily residing with her husband, namely Azizullah S/o Kodan Mahar (respondent No. 8). At the applicant's request, time was granted for a meeting with the alleged detainee on the Court premises. The meeting is accordingly arranged in the presence of the applicant and her husband. Even after such a meeting, the alleged detainee reiterated her stance and expressed her clear desire to reside with her husband, Azizullah alias Aziz. It is pertinent to mention that the applicant is the mother of the alleged detainee. Mr. Bakhshan Khan Mahar, Advocate, has filed Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents Nos. 6 to 8, which has been taken on record. The statements of the SHO, P.S. Khanpur Mahar, and ASI Muhammad Jaffer of P.S. Khanpur Mahar, along with the statement of the alleged lady and the memo of recovery, have also been submitted and are taken on record. In view of the above, particularly the categorical statement of the alleged detainee that she is living with her husband of her own free will and is glad to continue her matrimonial life, no case for interference is made out.

Khanpur Mahar, along with the statement of the alleged lady and the memo of recovery, have also been submitted and are taken on record. In view of the above, particularly the categorical statement of the alleged detainee that she is living with her husband of her own free will and is glad to continue her matrimonial life, no case for interference is made out. She has further stated that the applicant (her mother) and her father are not in favour of her marriage, and that the present application has been filed on that account. Accordingly, the instant application is dismissed. The alleged detainee lady, being sui juris, is at liberty to reside with her husband in accordance with her own free will. JUDGE
